                        <item>
            <title><![CDATA[Federal judge says she won't halt the immigration enforcement surge in Minnesota as a lawsuit proceeds]]></title>
            <link>https://www.pbs.org/newshour/nation/federal-judge-says-she-wont-halt-the-immigration-enforcement-surge-in-minnesota-as-a-lawsuit-proceeds</link>
            <description><![CDATA[A federal judge says she won't halt the immigration enforcement surge in Minnesota and the Twin Cities as a lawsuit over it proceeds.]]></description>
            <pubDate>Sat, 31 Jan 2026 18:07:21 -0500</pubDate>
            <guid isPermaLink="true">https://www.pbs.org/newshour/nation/federal-judge-says-she-wont-halt-the-immigration-enforcement-surge-in-minnesota-as-a-lawsuit-proceeds</guid>
                        <category domain="https://www.pbs.org/newshour/tag/immigration"><![CDATA[immigration]]></category>
                        <category domain="https://www.pbs.org/newshour/tag/immigration-enforcement"><![CDATA[immigration enforcement]]></category>
                        <category domain="https://www.pbs.org/newshour/tag/lawsuit"><![CDATA[lawsuit]]></category>
                        <category domain="https://www.pbs.org/newshour/tag/minnesota"><![CDATA[minnesota]]></category>
                                                        <dc:creator><![CDATA[Margery A. Beck, Associated Press]]></dc:creator>
                                <dc:creator><![CDATA[Jake Offenhartz, Associated Press]]></dc:creator>
                                                                <media:content url="https://d3i6fh83elv35t.cloudfront.net/static/2026/01/2026-01-31T142205Z_1227586173_RC2DCJAJ29NV_RTRMADP_3_USA-TRUMP-MINNESOTA-1024x683.jpg" medium="image" width="1024" height="683" />
                    </item>
                <item>
            <title><![CDATA[Judge orders release of 5-year-old Liam Conejo Ramos and his father from ICE detention]]></title>
            <link>https://www.pbs.org/newshour/nation/judge-orders-release-of-5-year-old-liam-conejo-ramos-and-his-father-from-ice-detention</link>
            <description><![CDATA[A 5-year-old boy and his father must be released by Tuesday from the Texas center where they've been held after being detained by immigration officers in Minnesota, a federal judge ordered Saturday.]]></description>
            <pubDate>Sat, 31 Jan 2026 17:41:22 -0500</pubDate>
            <guid isPermaLink="true">https://www.pbs.org/newshour/nation/judge-orders-release-of-5-year-old-liam-conejo-ramos-and-his-father-from-ice-detention</guid>
                        <category domain="https://www.pbs.org/newshour/tag/ice"><![CDATA[ice]]></category>
                        <category domain="https://www.pbs.org/newshour/tag/immigration"><![CDATA[immigration]]></category>
                        <category domain="https://www.pbs.org/newshour/tag/immigration-and-customs-enforcement"><![CDATA[immigration and customs enforcement]]></category>
                        <category domain="https://www.pbs.org/newshour/tag/minneapolis"><![CDATA[minneapolis]]></category>
                        <category domain="https://www.pbs.org/newshour/tag/minnesota"><![CDATA[minnesota]]></category>
                                                        <dc:creator><![CDATA[Geoff Mulvihill, Associated Press]]></dc:creator>
                                                                <media:content url="https://d3i6fh83elv35t.cloudfront.net/static/2026/01/2026-01-22T214535Z_511562760_RC225JALNI4I_RTRMADP_3_USA-TRUMP-MINNESOTA-CHILD-1024x662.jpg" medium="image" width="1024" height="662" />
                    </item>
                <item>
            <title><![CDATA[Major winter storm threatens East Coast including parts unaccustomed to heavy snow]]></title>
            <link>https://www.pbs.org/newshour/nation/major-winter-storm-threatens-east-coast-including-parts-unaccustomed-to-heavy-snow</link>
            <description><![CDATA[About 240 million people were under cold weather advisories and winter storm warnings Saturday as a powerful system threatened to bring winds, flooding and heavy snow to the East Coast.]]></description>
            <pubDate>Sat, 31 Jan 2026 16:35:08 -0500</pubDate>
            <guid isPermaLink="true">https://www.pbs.org/newshour/nation/major-winter-storm-threatens-east-coast-including-parts-unaccustomed-to-heavy-snow</guid>
                        <category domain="https://www.pbs.org/newshour/tag/bomb-cyclone"><![CDATA[bomb cyclone]]></category>
                        <category domain="https://www.pbs.org/newshour/tag/East-Coast"><![CDATA[East Coast]]></category>
                        <category domain="https://www.pbs.org/newshour/tag/southeast"><![CDATA[southeast]]></category>
                        <category domain="https://www.pbs.org/newshour/tag/weather"><![CDATA[Weather]]></category>
                        <category domain="https://www.pbs.org/newshour/tag/winter-storm"><![CDATA[Winter storm]]></category>
                                                        <dc:creator><![CDATA[Kristin M. Hall, Associated Press]]></dc:creator>
                                <dc:creator><![CDATA[Sophie Bates, Associated Press]]></dc:creator>
                                                                <media:content url="https://d3i6fh83elv35t.cloudfront.net/static/2026/01/2026-01-26T224633Z_921995814_RC299JAKVT2L_RTRMADP_3_USA-WEATHER-1024x683.jpg" medium="image" width="1024" height="683" />
                    </item>
                <item>
            <title><![CDATA[Here's what to know about the partial government shutdown and its impact]]></title>
            <link>https://www.pbs.org/newshour/nation/heres-what-to-know-about-the-partial-government-shutdown-and-its-impact</link>
            <description><![CDATA[Unlike the record shutdown in the fall, the partial shutdown that started Saturday may not last long.]]></description>
            <pubDate>Sat, 31 Jan 2026 14:23:54 -0500</pubDate>
            <guid isPermaLink="true">https://www.pbs.org/newshour/nation/heres-what-to-know-about-the-partial-government-shutdown-and-its-impact</guid>

                    </item>
                <item>
            <title><![CDATA[Artemis II highlights a shift in U.S. space strategy since Apollo and contrasts with China's closed program]]></title>
            <link>https://www.pbs.org/newshour/nation/artemis-ii-highlights-a-shift-in-u-s-space-strategy-since-apollo-and-contrasts-with-chinas-closed-program</link>
            <description><![CDATA[China's program is centrally directed and tightly controlled by the state, while the U.S. approach is intentionally open and designed so partners can operate within a shared framework.]]></description>
            <pubDate>Sat, 31 Jan 2026 13:30:10 -0500</pubDate>
            <guid isPermaLink="true">https://www.pbs.org/newshour/nation/artemis-ii-highlights-a-shift-in-u-s-space-strategy-since-apollo-and-contrasts-with-chinas-closed-program</guid>

                        <category domain="https://www.pbs.org/newshour/tag/artemis-ii"><![CDATA[Artemis II]]></category>
                        <category domain="https://www.pbs.org/newshour/tag/moon-mission"><![CDATA[moon mission]]></category>
                        <category domain="https://www.pbs.org/newshour/tag/nasa"><![CDATA[NASA]]></category>
                        <category domain="https://www.pbs.org/newshour/tag/space"><![CDATA[space]]></category>
                                                        <dc:creator><![CDATA[Michelle L.D. Hanlon, The Conversation]]></dc:creator>
                                                                <media:content url="https://d3i6fh83elv35t.cloudfront.net/static/2026/01/2026-01-17T155809Z_179429315_RC2Y2JAMAFPD_RTRMADP_3_SPACE-MOON-ARTEMIS-1024x683.jpg" medium="image" width="1024" height="683" />
                    </item>
                <item>
            <title><![CDATA[Israeli strikes in Gaza kill at least 30 Palestinians, including several children]]></title>
            <link>https://www.pbs.org/newshour/world/israeli-strikes-in-gaza-kill-at-least-30-palestinians-including-several-children</link>
            <description><![CDATA[Hospitals in Gaza said Israeli strikes killed at least 30 Palestinians including several children on Saturday, one of the highest tolls since the October ceasefire.]]></description>
            <pubDate>Sat, 31 Jan 2026 13:00:50 -0500</pubDate>
            <guid isPermaLink="true">https://www.pbs.org/newshour/world/israeli-strikes-in-gaza-kill-at-least-30-palestinians-including-several-children</guid>
                        <category domain="https://www.pbs.org/newshour/tag/gaza"><![CDATA[Gaza]]></category>
                        <category domain="https://www.pbs.org/newshour/tag/israel"><![CDATA[Israel]]></category>
                        <category domain="https://www.pbs.org/newshour/tag/israel-hamas-war"><![CDATA[Israel-Hamas war]]></category>
                                                        <dc:creator><![CDATA[Wafaa Shurafa, Associated Press]]></dc:creator>
                                <dc:creator><![CDATA[Samy Magdy, Associated Press]]></dc:creator>
                                <dc:creator><![CDATA[Sam Metz, Associated Press]]></dc:creator>
                                                                <media:content url="https://d3i6fh83elv35t.cloudfront.net/static/2026/01/2026-01-31T085651Z_1078429418_RC27CJAFLPZC_RTRMADP_3_ISRAEL-PALESTINIANS-GAZA-1024x688.jpg" medium="image" width="1024" height="688" />
                    </item>
                <item>
            <title><![CDATA[The latest Epstein files release includes famous names and new details about an earlier investigation]]></title>
            <link>https://www.pbs.org/newshour/nation/the-latest-epstein-files-release-includes-famous-names-and-new-details-about-an-earlier-investigation</link>
            <description><![CDATA[The documents released Friday include Epstein's communications with former White House advisers, an NFL team co-owner and billionaires including Bill Gates and Elon Musk.]]></description>
            <pubDate>Sat, 31 Jan 2026 12:30:50 -0500</pubDate>
            <guid isPermaLink="true">https://www.pbs.org/newshour/nation/the-latest-epstein-files-release-includes-famous-names-and-new-details-about-an-earlier-investigation</guid>
                        <category domain="https://www.pbs.org/newshour/tag/epstein-files"><![CDATA[epstein files]]></category>
                        <category domain="https://www.pbs.org/newshour/tag/epstein-files-transparency-act"><![CDATA[epstein files transparency act]]></category>
                        <category domain="https://www.pbs.org/newshour/tag/jeffrey-epstein"><![CDATA[jeffrey epstein]]></category>
                        <category domain="https://www.pbs.org/newshour/tag/u-s-justice-department"><![CDATA[U.S. Justice Department]]></category>
                                                        <dc:creator><![CDATA[Eric Tucker, Michael R. Sisak and Alanna Durkin Richer, Associated Press]]></dc:creator>
                                                                <media:content url="https://d3i6fh83elv35t.cloudfront.net/static/2025/12/2025-12-23T174227Z_2056904344_RC2CMIAWESLI_RTRMADP_3_USA-TRUMP-EPSTEIN-FILES-1024x726.jpg" medium="image" width="1024" height="726" />
                    </item>
                <item>
            <title><![CDATA[How bomb cyclones form and create hazardous conditions]]></title>
            <link>https://www.pbs.org/newshour/science/how-bomb-cyclones-form-and-create-hazardous-conditions</link>
            <description><![CDATA[Such rapidly strengthening storms are capable of producing heavy rain, blizzard conditions and intense winds that can create dangerous conditions such as downed trees and power outages.]]></description>
            <pubDate>Sat, 31 Jan 2026 11:35:19 -0500</pubDate>
            <guid isPermaLink="true">https://www.pbs.org/newshour/science/how-bomb-cyclones-form-and-create-hazardous-conditions</guid>
                        <category domain="https://www.pbs.org/newshour/tag/bomb-cyclone"><![CDATA[bomb cyclone]]></category>
                        <category domain="https://www.pbs.org/newshour/tag/weather"><![CDATA[Weather]]></category>
                        <category domain="https://www.pbs.org/newshour/tag/winter-storm"><![CDATA[Winter storm]]></category>
                                                        <dc:creator><![CDATA[Isabella O'Malley, Associated Press]]></dc:creator>

                    </item>
                <item>
            <title><![CDATA[Read the full indictment against Don Lemon, Georgia Fort and others charged in Minnesota]]></title>
            <link>https://www.pbs.org/newshour/nation/read-the-full-indictment-against-don-lemon-georgia-fort-and-others-charged-in-minnesota</link>
            <description><![CDATA[A grand jury indicted former CNN anchor Don Lemon, independent journalist Georgia Fort and others on charges of conspiracy and interfering with the First Amendment rights of worshippers during an anti-immigration enforcement protest that disrupted a Minnesota church service earlier this month.]]></description>
            <pubDate>Fri, 30 Jan 2026 20:01:09 -0500</pubDate>
            <guid isPermaLink="true">https://www.pbs.org/newshour/nation/read-the-full-indictment-against-don-lemon-georgia-fort-and-others-charged-in-minnesota</guid>
                        <category domain="https://www.pbs.org/newshour/tag/don-lemon"><![CDATA[Don lemon]]></category>
                        <category domain="https://www.pbs.org/newshour/tag/georgia-fort"><![CDATA[georgia fort]]></category>
                        <category domain="https://www.pbs.org/newshour/tag/indictments"><![CDATA[indictments]]></category>
                        <category domain="https://www.pbs.org/newshour/tag/minnesota"><![CDATA[minnesota]]></category>
                                                        <dc:creator><![CDATA[Associated Press]]></dc:creator>
                                                                <media:content url="https://d3i6fh83elv35t.cloudfront.net/static/2026/01/2026-01-30T233044Z_564796529_RC2ZBJANB4OP_RTRMADP_3_USA-TRUMP-MINNESOTA-LEMON-1024x763.jpg" medium="image" width="1024" height="763" />
                    </item>
                <item>
            <title><![CDATA[Iranian protesters share their stories of determination and dissent]]></title>
            <link>https://www.pbs.org/newshour/world/iranian-protesters-share-their-stories-of-determination-and-dissent</link>
            <description><![CDATA[These Iranians offer a rare glimpse of life in the Islamic Republic at a pivotal moment in its history.]]></description>
            <pubDate>Fri, 30 Jan 2026 19:25:35 -0500</pubDate>
            <guid isPermaLink="true">https://www.pbs.org/newshour/world/iranian-protesters-share-their-stories-of-determination-and-dissent</guid>
                        <category domain="https://www.pbs.org/newshour/tag/iran"><![CDATA[Iran]]></category>
                        <category domain="https://www.pbs.org/newshour/tag/iran-protests"><![CDATA[iran protests]]></category>
                        <category domain="https://www.pbs.org/newshour/tag/rial"><![CDATA[rial]]></category>
                                                        <dc:creator><![CDATA[Kareem Chehayeb, Associated Press]]></dc:creator>
                                <dc:creator><![CDATA[Sarah El Deeb, Associated Press]]></dc:creator>
                                                                <media:content url="https://d3i6fh83elv35t.cloudfront.net/static/2026/01/2026-01-26T164043Z_485114348_RC2Q7JAYZAB6_RTRMADP_3_IRAN-ECONOMY-PROTESTS-UN-1024x763.jpg" medium="image" width="1024" height="763" />
                    </item>
                <item>
            <title><![CDATA[After years of transformation, twin towns once run by FLDS sect enjoy new freedoms]]></title>
            <link>https://www.pbs.org/newshour/nation/after-years-of-transformation-twin-towns-once-run-by-flds-sect-enjoy-new-freedoms</link>
            <description><![CDATA[The prairie dresses, walled compounds and distrust of outsiders that were once hallmarks of two towns on the Arizona-Utah border are mostly gone. These days, Colorado City, Arizona, and neighboring Hildale, Utah, look much like any other town in this remote and picturesque area near Zion National Park.]]></description>
            <pubDate>Fri, 30 Jan 2026 19:19:36 -0500</pubDate>
            <guid isPermaLink="true">https://www.pbs.org/newshour/nation/after-years-of-transformation-twin-towns-once-run-by-flds-sect-enjoy-new-freedoms</guid>
                        <category domain="https://www.pbs.org/newshour/tag/arizona"><![CDATA[arizona]]></category>
                        <category domain="https://www.pbs.org/newshour/tag/flds"><![CDATA[flds]]></category>
                        <category domain="https://www.pbs.org/newshour/tag/fundamentalist-church-of-jesus-christ-of-latter-day-saints"><![CDATA[Fundamentalist Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-Day Saints]]></category>
                        <category domain="https://www.pbs.org/newshour/tag/utah"><![CDATA[utah]]></category>
                                                        <dc:creator><![CDATA[Jacques Billeaud, Associated Press]]></dc:creator>
                                                                <media:content url="https://d3i6fh83elv35t.cloudfront.net/static/2026/01/2017-05-08T000000Z_1991952892_RC141D606D90_RTRMADP_3_UTAH-CHURCH-COMPOUND-1024x683.jpg" medium="image" width="1024" height="683" />
                    </item>
                <item>
            <title><![CDATA[What to expect in Texas' 18th Congressional District special runoff election]]></title>
            <link>https://www.pbs.org/newshour/politics/what-to-expect-in-texas-18th-congressional-district-special-runoff-election</link>
            <description><![CDATA[With only two Democrats on the ballot, the party is guaranteed to at least temporarily narrow the slim Republican majority in the House.]]></description>
            <pubDate>Fri, 30 Jan 2026 19:05:40 -0500</pubDate>
            <guid isPermaLink="true">https://www.pbs.org/newshour/politics/what-to-expect-in-texas-18th-congressional-district-special-runoff-election</guid>

                        <category domain="https://www.pbs.org/newshour/tag/vote-2026"><![CDATA[Vote 2026]]></category>
                                                        <dc:creator><![CDATA[Robert Yoon, Associated Press]]></dc:creator>
                                                                <media:content url="https://d3i6fh83elv35t.cloudfront.net/static/2025/11/GettyImages-2245317112-1024x683.jpg" medium="image" width="1024" height="683" />
                    </item>
                <item>
            <title><![CDATA[Arrests of journalists fuel backlash as anti-ICE protests spread from Minneapolis]]></title>
            <link>https://www.pbs.org/newshour/show/arrests-of-journalists-fuel-backlash-as-anti-ice-protests-spread-from-minneapolis</link>
            <description><![CDATA[The Justice Department said today it has opened a civil rights investigation into the shooting death of Alex Pretti, the ICU nurse who was killed in Minneapolis last weekend. That news came shortly after there were more arrests in Minnesota over a protest, and as the national backlash against the immigration crackdown grows. Special correspondent Fred de Sam Lazaro reports.]]></description>
            <pubDate>Fri, 30 Jan 2026 18:55:36 -0500</pubDate>
            <guid isPermaLink="true">https://www.pbs.org/newshour/show/arrests-of-journalists-fuel-backlash-as-anti-ice-protests-spread-from-minneapolis</guid>
                        <category domain="https://www.pbs.org/newshour/tag/don-lemon"><![CDATA[Don lemon]]></category>
                        <category domain="https://www.pbs.org/newshour/tag/donald-trump"><![CDATA[Donald Trump news]]></category>
                        <category domain="https://www.pbs.org/newshour/tag/ice"><![CDATA[ice]]></category>
                        <category domain="https://www.pbs.org/newshour/tag/immigration-enforcement"><![CDATA[immigration enforcement]]></category>
                        <category domain="https://www.pbs.org/newshour/tag/minneapolis"><![CDATA[minneapolis]]></category>
                        <category domain="https://www.pbs.org/newshour/tag/minnesota"><![CDATA[minnesota]]></category>
                                                        <dc:creator><![CDATA[Fred de Sam Lazaro]]></dc:creator>
                                <dc:creator><![CDATA[Kayan Taraporevala]]></dc:creator>
                                                                <media:content url="https://d3i6fh83elv35t.cloudfront.net/static/2026/01/mncrackdown-1024x683.jpg" medium="image" width="1024" height="683" />
                    </item>
                <item>
            <title><![CDATA[What Trump's nomination of inflation hawk Kevin Warsh means for the Federal Reserve]]></title>
            <link>https://www.pbs.org/newshour/show/what-trumps-nomination-of-inflation-hawk-kevin-warsh-means-for-the-federal-reserve</link>
            <description><![CDATA[President Trump announced Kevin Warsh as his pick to be the next chairman of the Federal Reserve. Warsh was an advisor to George W. Bush and served on the Federal Reserve's Board of Governors from 2006 to 2011. If confirmed, Warsh will succeed current Chair Jerome Powell, whose term ends in May. Amna Nawaz discussed more with David Wessel.]]></description>
            <pubDate>Fri, 30 Jan 2026 18:50:11 -0500</pubDate>
            <guid isPermaLink="true">https://www.pbs.org/newshour/show/what-trumps-nomination-of-inflation-hawk-kevin-warsh-means-for-the-federal-reserve</guid>
                        <category domain="https://www.pbs.org/newshour/tag/donald-trump"><![CDATA[Donald Trump news]]></category>
                        <category domain="https://www.pbs.org/newshour/tag/federal-reserve"><![CDATA[Federal reserve]]></category>
                        <category domain="https://www.pbs.org/newshour/tag/interest-rates"><![CDATA[interest rates]]></category>
                        <category domain="https://www.pbs.org/newshour/tag/jerome-powell"><![CDATA[Jerome Powell]]></category>
                        <category domain="https://www.pbs.org/newshour/tag/kevin-warsh"><![CDATA[Kevin Warsh]]></category>
                                                        <dc:creator><![CDATA[Amna Nawaz]]></dc:creator>
                                <dc:creator><![CDATA[Diane Lincoln Estes]]></dc:creator>
                                                                <media:content url="https://d3i6fh83elv35t.cloudfront.net/static/2026/01/trumpspick-1024x683.jpg" medium="image" width="1024" height="683" />
                    </item>
                <item>
            <title><![CDATA[News Wrap: Mangione will not face death penalty after federal murder charge dismissed]]></title>
            <link>https://www.pbs.org/newshour/show/news-wrap-mangione-will-not-face-death-penalty-after-federal-murder-charge-dismissed</link>
            <description><![CDATA[In our news wrap Friday, a judge ruled that federal prosecutors cannot seek the death penalty against Luigi Mangione and dismissed murder and weapons charges, nearly 230,000 homes and businesses across the South have now gone six straight days without electricity after last weekend's winter storm and Russia says its temporary halt on attacking Ukraine's capital will only last until Sunday.]]></description>
            <pubDate>Fri, 30 Jan 2026 18:45:58 -0500</pubDate>
            <guid isPermaLink="true">https://www.pbs.org/newshour/show/news-wrap-mangione-will-not-face-death-penalty-after-federal-murder-charge-dismissed</guid>
                        <category domain="https://www.pbs.org/newshour/tag/news-wrap"><![CDATA[news wrap]]></category>
                                                            <media:content url="https://d3i6fh83elv35t.cloudfront.net/static/2026/01/newswrap-25-1024x768.jpg" medium="image" width="1024" height="768" />
                    </item>
                <item>
            <title><![CDATA[This ancient pledge is made whenever Olympic Games are held]]></title>
            <link>https://www.pbs.org/newshour/world/this-ancient-pledge-is-made-whenever-olympic-games-are-held</link>
            <description><![CDATA[The Milan Cortina Winter Olympics begin in one week, and the United Nations and organizers are calling for a 7-week pause of all wars worldwide — as they do every time the Olympics take place.]]></description>
            <pubDate>Fri, 30 Jan 2026 18:45:05 -0500</pubDate>
            <guid isPermaLink="true">https://www.pbs.org/newshour/world/this-ancient-pledge-is-made-whenever-olympic-games-are-held</guid>

                <item>
            <title><![CDATA[What's revealed in the latest Epstein files release – and what's redacted]]></title>
            <link>https://www.pbs.org/newshour/show/whats-revealed-in-the-latest-epstein-files-release-and-whats-redacted</link>
            <description><![CDATA[The Department of Justice released its latest, and likely last, batch of files related to investigations into Jeffrey Epstein. It comes more than a month after the missed deadline set by Congress. The release includes more than 3 million pages, 2,000 videos and 180,000 images. The DOJ says there are redactions, but has not provided a full explanation of what is excluded. Ali Rogin reports.]]></description>
            <pubDate>Fri, 30 Jan 2026 18:35:54 -0500</pubDate>
            <guid isPermaLink="true">https://www.pbs.org/newshour/show/whats-revealed-in-the-latest-epstein-files-release-and-whats-redacted</guid>

                                                        <dc:creator><![CDATA[Ali Schmitz]]></dc:creator>
                                <dc:creator><![CDATA[David Bentley]]></dc:creator>

                    </item>
                <item>
            <title><![CDATA[Remembering the life and career of Catherine O'Hara]]></title>
            <link>https://www.pbs.org/newshour/show/remembering-the-life-and-career-of-catherine-ohara</link>
            <description><![CDATA[The entertainment world is mourning the loss of one of its comedy greats. Catherine O'Hara died Friday at the age of 71. She leaves behind a body of work that ranges from cult classics, to blockbuster films, and some small-screen gems. Geoff Bennett reports.]]></description>

            <title><![CDATA[FBI raid of election offices ignites debate over voting security and federal authority]]></title>
            <link>https://www.pbs.org/newshour/show/fbi-raid-of-election-offices-ignites-debate-over-voting-security-and-federal-authority</link>
            <description><![CDATA[The FBI's move to execute a warrant at voting offices in Fulton County, Georgia, and the presence of Director of National Intelligence Tulsi Gabbard have ignited a fierce debate over election security and federal authority. Geoff Bennett discussed more with Rick Hasen, professor of law and political science at UCLA and author of "A Real Right to Vote."]]></description>

                <item>
            <title><![CDATA[How people with disabilities could bear the burden of Medicaid funding cuts]]></title>
            <link>https://www.pbs.org/newshour/show/how-people-with-disabilities-will-bear-the-burden-of-medicaid-funding-cuts</link>
            <description><![CDATA[According to the nonpartisan Congressional Budget Office, President Trump's One Big Beautiful Bill Act will slash more than a trillion dollars in federal spending from Medicaid and the Children's Health Insurance Program by 2034. Judy Woodruff examines what those cuts could mean for home and community care providers who serve adults with special needs. It's part of her series, Disability Reframed.]]></description>
            <pubDate>Fri, 30 Jan 2026 18:25:44 -0500</pubDate>
